rationale: Pinned 2b4fb8f0 against explicit base 71398a68. Finding A is resolved: the dated addendum is forward-only, expressly supersedes every stale discharge/re-tally family across the spec, and accurately matches code, ADR-0037, and consolidated-round-3.md: self-contained pending marker, satisfy-from-marker before close on every path, no panel re-read or discharge. Finding B remains a real pre-existing FinalizeEpic bypass, but the follow-up disposition satisfies me and it no longer blocks 114. R1/R2 and the stated user outcome are explicitly scoped to mindspec complete; the new scope note does not over-claim, identifies the exact separate verb/path, and records immediate P1 hardening as mindspec-o4fd. Deferring that distinct lifecycle hardening is therefore honest scope control, not acceptance of a defect in 114's shipped mechanism. Round delta contains no .go files. In the absolute /tmp clone: go build ./... passed, gofmt output was empty, and all four requested package tests passed.

findings.detail: The superseding spec and plan addenda accurately document the shipped no-discharge design and unambiguously override the preserved stale text.
The bypass remains real, but its disposition as an explicitly disclosed, separate-verb, pre-existing out-of-scope P1 follow-up (mindspec-o4fd) is acceptable for merging complete-scoped spec 114.
